acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Daemon startup wires replication server + manager and registers channels to the coordinator.
- [ ] Inbound replication events reach the coordinator through bounded queues and produce ACKs.
- [ ] Outbound sessions receive events via broadcaster or WAL range fallback.
- [ ] Overload path rejects replication ingest (no unbounded buffering).
description:
**Problem**
This bead is now the integration glue for replication runtime. Sessions/manager/server are split into separate beads; we need to wire them into the daemon coordinator with bounded channels, correct defaults, and backpressure so replication traffic is serialized safely.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md sections 7.5, 9.1-9.8, 7.2 (store runtime wiring)
- Split beads: bd-3m5.46 (session), bd-3m5.47 (manager), bd-3m5.48 (server)
**Files:** src/daemon/repl/mod.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/run.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a repl module root to share wiring/config between session/manager/server.
- On daemon startup, spawn replication server + manager threads and register bounded channels into the coordinator.
- Route inbound replication ingest through the coordinator (WAL append + apply) and produce ACKs via session channel.
- Route outbound fanout from EventBroadcaster (with WAL fallback) and enforce AdmissionController limits at ingress.
title: Phase 5: Replication runtime integration

description:
**Problem**
Daemon must shut down gracefully without losing durable events. Operators need tracing and metrics.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (graceful shutdown, observability requirements)

**Design**
- On SIGTERM: stop accepting mutations, drain in-flight durability waits (bounded), flush WAL, close segments, exit
- Every mutation traceable via txn_id and client_request_id
- Replication sessions log peer identity and namespace authorization
- Emit counters: wal_append_ok/err, wal_fsync_ok/err, apply_ok/err, repl_events_in/out, checkpoint_export_ok/err
- Emit gauges: IPC inflight, repl queue depth, per-peer lag
- Emit histograms: wal_append_duration, wal_fsync_duration, apply_duration, checkpoint_duration

**Acceptance**
- [ ] SIGTERM triggers graceful drain and flush
- [ ] Metrics emitted via tracing or metrics crate
- [ ] Tests verify no event loss on shutdown
- [ ] Tests verify metric emission

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/metrics.rs (new), Cargo.toml (tracing/metrics)
title: Phase 4: Graceful shutdown + observability metrics

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] identity_handshake model is extended or a new cross-lane model added (e.g., beads_stateright_models/examples/identity_epoch_machine.rs)
- [ ] Properties cover: no cross-store merge, no cross-epoch merge, and consistent rejection across replication/checkpoint/WAL paths (todo.md Model #6)
- [ ] Model doc comments reference REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.1, §2.1, §9.3, and §9.10
- [ ] Example runs via cargo run --example identity_epoch_machine (or equivalent)
description:
**Problem**
Identity and epoch mismatch rules must be enforced consistently across replication, checkpoint import, and WAL header validation (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.1, §2.1, §9.3, §9.10). The current identity_handshake model only covers the replication handshake path.

**Design Notes**
We need one model or a paired model set that proves the same guardrails apply in all lanes.
design:
**Design**
Extend or add a Stateright model that covers store_id and store_epoch gating across: (1) replication HELLO/WELCOME, (2) checkpoint import, and (3) WAL segment header validation. Model mismatches and assert hard-close or reject semantics in all lanes (Plan §9.10 error handling).

Reuse or build on beads_stateright_models/examples/identity_handshake.rs, but add explicit actions for checkpoint import and WAL header accept/reject. Ensure the same validation function is referenced in comments to map to the plan.
title: Stateright: store identity + epoch guardrails across lanes

description:
**Problem**
`git/wire.rs` repeatedly does `String::from_utf8(bytes.to_vec())` for blobs. Avoidable copy.

**Design**
Use `std::str::from_utf8(bytes)?` and parse from the slice, or stream JSONL via `serde_json::Deserializer`.

Not a moral issue, just free perf + less churn.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No unnecessary `.to_vec()` in blob parsing
- [ ] Benchmark shows improvement (or at least no regression)

**Files:** src/git/wire.rs
title: Wire parsing does extra allocations

closed_reason: Added serde boundary validation via try_from/into for BeadId, ActorId, NoteId, and Priority. Invalid values now fail at deserialization rather than scattered through handlers. Added 6 tests for the new validation behavior.
title: Add serde boundary validation for identity types (BeadId, ActorId, NoteId, Priority)

description:
**Problem**
CLI logs/errors don’t carry command context, making it hard to correlate failures with user commands or repos.

**Design**
- Wrap `cli::run` in a `tracing::info_span!("cli_command", command = ..., repo = ...)` in `src/bin/main.rs` or `src/cli/mod.rs`.
- Extract command name and repo (if present) from `cli::Args`.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] CLI errors are emitted within a `cli_command` span that includes command + repo fields.
- [ ] No behavior change for stdout/stderr output.

**Files:** src/bin/main.rs, src/cli/mod.rs
title: Observability: CLI command span + context

description:
**Problem**

Go beads has async coordination gates that block workflow steps until conditions are met. Gate evaluation commands check gate conditions and auto-close resolved gates.

Missing from beads-rs:
- `bd gate list` - List open gates
- `bd gate check` - Evaluate gates and close resolved ones
- `bd gate resolve` - Manually close a gate
- `bd gate show` - Show gate details
- Gate type filtering (--type=gh:run, gh:pr, timer, bead)
- Auto-discovery of GitHub run IDs by workflow name

Gate types (from gate.go):
- human: Requires manual bd close
- timer: Expires after timeout
- gh:run: Waits for GitHub Actions workflow
- gh:pr: Waits for PR merge
- bead: Waits for cross-rig bead to close

**Design**

Implement gate evaluation commands using the gate bead infrastructure.

Commands:

1. `bd gate list` - List gates
   - `--all` - Include closed gates
   - `--limit=N` - Limit results
   - Filter: type=gate, exclude status=closed (unless --all)

2. `bd gate check` - Evaluate and auto-close gates
   - `--type=<type>` - Filter by gate type (gh, gh:run, gh:pr, timer, bead, all)
   - `--dry-run` - Show what would happen without changes
   - `--escalate` - Escalate failed/expired gates
   - `--limit=N` - Limit gates to check

3. `bd gate resolve <id>` - Manually close a gate
   - `--reason=<text>` - Provide close reason
   - Equivalent to `bd close <id>` but gate-specific

4. `bd gate show <id>` - Show gate details
   - Display: await_type, await_id, timeout, waiters, status
   - Validates issue is type=gate

Gate evaluation logic (from gate.go checkGHRun, checkGHPR, checkTimer, checkBeadGate):

**gh:run gates:**
- Query: `gh run view <run-id> --json status,conclusion`
- Resolved: status=completed AND conclusion=success
- Escalated: status=completed AND conclusion in (failure, canceled)
- Auto-discovery: If await_id is workflow name (non-numeric), query most recent run

**gh:pr gates:**
- Query: `gh pr view <pr-id> --json state,merged`
- Resolved: state=MERGED
- Escalated: state=CLOSED AND merged=false

**timer gates:**
- Resolved: now > created_at + timeout
- Never escalated (just expire)

**bead gates:**
- Format: await_id = "<rig>:<bead-id>" (e.g., "gastown:gt-abc123")
- Resolved: target bead status=closed
- Query cross-rig database (routing.ResolveBeadsDirForRig)

Auto-discovery for gh:run (from discoverRunIDByWorkflowName):
```rust
// If await_id is workflow name hint (non-numeric):
// 1. Query: gh run list --workflow=<name> --json=databaseId,status --limit=5
// 2. Take most recent run (first in list)
// 3. Update gate's await_id with discovered run ID
```

Implementation files:
- `src/cli/gate/list.rs` - List gates command
- `src/cli/gate/check.rs` - Gate evaluation command
- `src/cli/gate/resolve.rs` - Manual gate resolution
- `src/cli/gate/show.rs` - Show gate details
- `src/daemon/ops.rs` - Gate evaluation operations
- `src/daemon/gate_eval.rs` - Gate type-specific evaluation logic
- `src/daemon/github.rs` - GitHub CLI integration (gh run view, gh pr view)

**Design Notes**

Gate evaluation requires external tools:
- `gh` CLI for GitHub gates (gh:run, gh:pr)
- Cross-rig database access for bead gates
- Local time for timer gates

Error handling:
- gh CLI not found → skip GitHub gates, warn user
- gh CLI auth issues → report error but don't crash
- Cross-rig database not found → warn but continue

Dry-run mode (--dry-run):
- Show what would happen without making changes
- Format: "✓ bd-abc: would resolve - workflow 'CI' succeeded"

Escalation (--escalate):
- Failed/expired gates can be escalated to create alerts
- Call `gt escalate` if available (Gas Town integration)
- Optional feature, not required for core functionality

**Acceptance**

- [ ] `bd gate list` shows open gates with type, await_id, timeout
- [ ] `bd gate list --all` includes closed gates
- [ ] `bd gate check` evaluates all open gates
- [ ] `bd gate check --type=gh:run` filters to GitHub run gates
- [ ] `bd gate check --dry-run` shows evaluation results without changes
- [ ] gh:run evaluation queries GitHub Actions and closes on success
- [ ] gh:pr evaluation queries PRs and closes on merge
- [ ] timer evaluation checks created_at + timeout
- [ ] bead evaluation checks cross-rig bead status
- [ ] Auto-discovery for gh:run with workflow name hint
- [ ] `bd gate resolve <id>` manually closes a gate
- [ ] `bd gate show <id>` displays gate details
- [ ] Tests for gate evaluation logic (mock gh CLI responses)
- [ ] Integration test for full gate workflow

**Files:**
- src/cli/gate/list.rs
- src/cli/gate/check.rs
- src/cli/gate/resolve.rs
- src/cli/gate/show.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/gate_eval.rs
- src/daemon/github.rs
title: Gate evaluation commands (bd gate check, list, resolve)

description:
**Problem**
beads is currently shipped primarily as the bd package and assembly crate. We need a deliberate downstream library surface for Rust, with future room for simpler Python/TS query-view consumers, without regrowing beads-rs into an umbrella crate.

**Design**
Create a new downstream facade crate for Rust, keep beads-rs assembly-only, and explicitly decide the fate of the current Go-compat export path. Break implementation into follow-up tasks after the design/spec is approved.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Approved design spec for Rust v1 SDK shape
- [ ] Follow-up implementation tasks captured in beads
- [ ] Crate-boundary story remains truthful

**Files:** docs/superpowers/specs, docs/superpowers/plans, crates/beads, crates/beads-daemon, crates/beads-rs, docs/CRATE_DAG.md
title: Library-first downstream surface
